pattern: Count character frequencies, sort characters by descending frequency, then concatenate each character repeated by its count.
hints: Count how many times each character appears in the input string.
Sort the unique characters in descending order of their frequency; ties can be broken arbitrarily.
Build the result by repeating each character exactly its frequency number of times in that sorted order.

edge_inputs.description: tree: e appears twice, r and t once — e must come first
cccaaa: both letters appear 3 times, either order valid
Aabb: b appears twice, A and a appear once, uppercase treated separately
all same character — output is identical to input
string with digits — digits are valid characters and sorted by frequency
mix of uppercase, lowercase, digits with varying frequencies

stress.description: long string of random lowercase letters
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(size))
    return [s]
